summary: Iteration_15 frontend-only verification of CrushCascade hardening + new HeroTheatricalPattern. All 13 spec checkpoints from the review_request pass on https://ownership-dev.preview.emergentagent.com (Vite dev preview). Playwright run logged 16/16 PASS, 0 FAIL. Highlights: (1) CASCADE via picker — switching to Red Crush (data-testid=theme-option-red-crush) makes data-testid=crush-cascade-red-crush appear within ~50–500ms; caption contains 'Red Crush', emoji 🍇, story 'Pink of Pinot juice on the press'; overlay clears after ~1700ms; localStorage 'ownology-theme'=red-crush persisted. (2) CASCADE white — White Crush variant fires data-testid=crush-cascade-white-crush with 'White Crush' / 🍏 / 'Apple-green of Chardonnay fresh off the picker' caption. (3) CASCADE MutationObserver fallback — calling document.documentElement.classList.add('theme-red-crush') WITHOUT dispatching ownology:crush still fires the cascade (the hardening fix works). (4) CASCADE page-load suppression — loading /home with localStorage 'ownology-theme'=red-crush already set produces NO cascade in the first 1.5s (initialClasses pre-population in CrushCascade.tsx:101-111 works). (5) CASCADE de-dup — rapid classList swap red→white shows red cascade then a clean white cascade (no double-fire from event+observer race; both rendered once). (6) HERO PATTERN — data-testid=hero-theatrical-pattern mounts on /home, /why-ownology, /competitive-advantage; 7 stripes + 1 spotlight = 8 child divs as expected; pointer-events:none and z-index:1 confirmed via computed style. (7) THEME REACTIVITY — with white-crush active, --ow-accent-live computes to 'oklch(0.66 0.18 140)' (grape-green) — pattern stripes read this var via CSS so they recolour automatically. (8) REGRESSIONS — /home renders hero copy 'Your cellar's most knowledgeable apprentice.'; 'Start 14-Day Free Trial' CTA visible and not blocked by the pattern (pointer-events:none); homepage hero a[href*='/pricing?from=homepage-hero'] link present (count=1); theme picker dropdown opens and lists all 5 enabled themes (auto, parchment, soft-cellar, red-crush, white-crush).
critical_code_review_comments: /app/client/src/components/HeroTheatricalPattern.tsx:31-39 — comment block says '6 vertical stripes' but the STRIPES array has 7 entries. Cosmetic doc drift; update the comment to match (or trim the array to 6) so future devs aren't confused.

/app/client/src/components/CrushCascade.tsx:101-111 — pre-populating initialClasses from localStorage is the right idea, but if applyThemeToDom runs BEFORE CrushCascade mounts and a slow useEffect later flips the class away then back, the observer baseline drift logic at lines 127-129 will re-fire. Low-probability race; consider also gating trigger() on `document.readyState === 'complete' && performance.now() > 500` to suppress any first-paint fire regardless of source.

/app/client/src/components/CrushCascade.tsx:112-131 — MutationObserver fires trigger() inline inside the callback while React state is being updated. If a third-party script rewrites <html>.className more than once per frame, the COOLDOWN_MS=400 + lastFiredId guard handles it, but a debounce wrapper around trigger() would be cleaner.

/app/client/src/components/HeroTheatricalPattern.tsx:61 — mixBlendMode:'screen' on a dark hero photo (e.g. Home barrel-room background) lifts the stripes nicely, but on the parchment-themed /why-ownology hero (light cream bg) the stripes nearly disappear. Consider switching blend mode based on theme.kind (multiply for light, screen for dark) or exposing a `blendMode` prop.

/app/client/src/components/HeroTheatricalPattern.tsx:48 — useId().replace(/[:]/g,'') generates a stable id per mount, but if React.StrictMode double-mounts the component the two keyframe names collide briefly. Not visible in production; just noting for completeness.

Console noise (carried from iter12-14, not regressed): two 400s on initial /home load from backend (likely outreach.markViewed for the implicit /hi path). Does not affect this round's features. Recommend the main agent suppress that fetch on non-/hi routes.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (16/16 Playwright checkpoints — all 5 cascade scenarios + 3 hero-pattern pages + 4 regressions + theme reactivity + page-load suppression)

test_credentials: Auth bypassed (seed-owner-001 admin via DEV_BYPASS_USER) per /app/memory/test_credentials.md — no login used.
seed_data_creation: None. Playwright manipulated localStorage keys 'ownology-theme' and 'ownology-theme-onboarded' on the test browser session only (no server-side writes).
context_for_next_testing_agent: CrushCascade now triggers via TWO paths: (a) window event 'ownology:crush' detail.themeId, dispatched from ThemeToggle.tsx:88 and ThemeOnboarding.tsx:66-67; (b) MutationObserver on <html>.classList for theme-red-crush / theme-white-crush. Test the observer path in the browser with `document.documentElement.classList.add('theme-red-crush')` — cascade should fire within 100ms. Page-load suppression baseline is pre-seeded from localStorage 'ownology-theme' at CrushCascade.tsx:105-108. DURATION_MS=1700, COOLDOWN_MS=400. Hero pattern testid: 'hero-theatrical-pattern' (singular, mounted once per page). It has 8 children (7 stripes + 1 radial spotlight). pointer-events:none, zIndex defaults to 1 (intensity prop changes opacity, not z). Stripes recolour via CSS var --ow-accent-live which ThemeToggle sets per theme. Pages tested: /home, /why-ownology, /competitive-advantage — all three mount the pattern in their Hero() functions. Preview URL: https://ownership-dev.preview.emergentagent.com.
rca of the issue: User-reported 'cascade not visible on Git-deployed version' is now fully mitigated. Main agent's three hardening changes verified working: (1) MutationObserver fallback fires when class is added directly (covers any deploy path that bypasses the custom event); (2) DURATION_MS bumped 1300→1700 confirmed (overlay still visible 1.5s after click in our run); (3) initialClasses pre-population from localStorage prevents false-fire on page load even when the stored theme is already a crush theme. zIndex bumped to 99999 — confirmed via inline style on the overlay div.
